titleStmt.title: SII 32, appendix, no. 1: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 1. (A.R. No. B of 2008-09.) UTTIRAMĒRŪR, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the base, south wall, Kailāsanātha temple.
This incomplete inscription on the grounds of palaeography can be assigned to the 9th century A.D. Hence it may belong to the reign of Āditya I.

It records an agreement made by the members of the garden committee with Dēvandaidēvaṉai Diraṉ, (a resident) of Northern bazaar, who made a gift of lampstand, to the god of the big temple of Mugappe Brahmīśvaram and after his disappearance (expiry ?) (ivaṉ illaiyāṉa piṟagū) the above committee received from his son, Ādittaṉ nine kaḻañjū of gold weighed by the stone (eḍar pūram) and undertook the responsibility of measūring oūt an uḻakkū of oil daily (for būrning the lamp).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kovirājakecariparmmakku ṭu toṭṭavārika perumakkaḷ Eḻuttu . vaṭakkil Aṅkāṭi tevantai tevāṉaitiraṉ Olaiyāvatiṉ mukappe brahmiśvarattu periyataḷi mahādevarkku vaitta nilaiviḷakkoṉṟukkum Ivaṉ ṉillai
ṉa piṟaku Ivaṉ makaṉ ĀtititaṉRead ātittaṉpakkal Ivaṉṉilai viḷakku 1 ku Eḷarpuram niṟai Oṉpatiṉ kaḻañce poṉ koṇṭu Ivviḷakku candrātityaruḷḷaḷavuThe letter vu is found engraved in smaller form between the letter ḷa and mu m muṭṭāmai toṭṭavāriyuRead toṭṭa vāriyamperumakka ḷome nicati Uḻakkeṇṇaiyā paṭinaṭaiyar mel poṉ
Digital edition of SII 32, appendix, no. 1 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
